title: Add secretx-systemd-creds backend
description:
systemd v250+ provides a clean secrets mechanism for headless daemons via LoadCredentialEncrypted= in unit files. At service start, systemd decrypts credentials (via TPM2 or host key) into a per-service tmpfs at $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY. The plaintext is never written to disk and is only visible to that service process.

A secretx-systemd-creds backend would:
- URI: secretx:systemd-creds:<name> resolves to $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/<name>
- Return SecretError::Unavailable if $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY is not set (not running under systemd)
- Be a thin wrapper — the decryption is done by systemd before exec, so get() is just a file read

The secretx-file backend already works for this use case if you point it at the full path. The value of a dedicated backend is URI ergonomics and a clear error message when run outside systemd.

Design notes:
- No daemon needed at runtime
- Encrypted at rest (TPM2-bound or host-key-bound via systemd-creds encrypt)
- Decrypted to tmpfs at service start — never hits disk as plaintext
- Scope-isolated: CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY only visible to the specific service unit
- Requires systemd v250+ (2022); check distro support before committing
notes: Related to discussion about linux-native kernel keyring vs desktop keyring vs systemd credentials. secretx-file already handles this use case functionally. A dedicated crate adds URI ergonomics and self-documenting errors only.
